  "clarification_question": "Which focus would you like: (A) sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity and tone only, (B) assessment of the paragraph’s logic and whether its claims are supported by the reported results, or (C) higher-level restructuring/reframing of the paragraph’s thesis and ordering?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The paragraph advances a thesis and supporting steps, so checking internal argument coherence is a central expectation.",
    "R1.3": "It reports quantitative outcomes and claims benefits, making claim-to-evidence alignment a central responsibility.",
    "R1.6": "A vague request to “take a look” at a paragraph commonly implies sentence-level clarity and flow improvements.",
    "RX.1": "Any assessment should note uncertainty given limited context from a single paragraph.",
    "RX.2": "Feedback must avoid amplifying or endorsing overclaims beyond what the paragraph evidence warrants.",
    "RX.3": "Work should strictly target this paragraph and not drift into broader paper-wide or external evaluations.",
    "RX.4": "Guidance should avoid encouraging misleading representations of system performance or enterprise data practices.",
    "RX.5": "Comments should be traceable to specific sentences or claims in the provided paragraph without introducing outside facts."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus would you like: (A) sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity and tone only, (B) assessment of the paragraph’s logic and whether its claims are supported by the reported results, or (C) higher-level restructuring/reframing of the paragraph’s thesis and ordering?

rationale.R1.2: The paragraph advances a thesis and supporting steps, so checking internal argument coherence is a central expectation.
rationale.R1.3: It reports quantitative outcomes and claims benefits, making claim-to-evidence alignment a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A vague request to “take a look” at a paragraph commonly implies sentence-level clarity and flow improvements.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessment should note uncertainty given limited context from a single paragraph.
rationale.RX.2: Feedback must avoid amplifying or endorsing overclaims beyond what the paragraph evidence warrants.
rationale.RX.3: Work should strictly target this paragraph and not drift into broader paper-wide or external evaluations.
rationale.RX.4: Guidance should avoid encouraging misleading representations of system performance or enterprise data practices.
rationale.RX.5: Comments should be traceable to specific sentences or claims in the provided paragraph without introducing outside facts.
